Examples of even functions include
then f is odd if the following equation holds for all x and -x in the domain of f

annotations.label: Integral of an odd function over symmetric interval

anchor.snippet: The integral of an odd function from − A to + A is zero

annotations.label: Integral of an even function over symmetric interval

anchor.snippet: The integral of an even function from − A to + A is twice the integral from 0 to + A

annotations.label: Maclaurin series of an even function

anchor.snippet: The Maclaurin series of an even function includes only even powers.

annotations.label: Maclaurin series of an odd function

anchor.snippet: The Maclaurin series of an odd function includes only odd powers.

annotations.label: Fourier series of a periodic even function

anchor.snippet: The Fourier series of a periodic even function includes only cosine terms.

annotations.label: Fourier series of a periodic odd function

anchor.snippet: The Fourier series of a periodic odd function includes only sine terms.
a symmetric matrix is a square matrix that is equal to its transpose

annotations.label: Only square matrices can be symmetric

anchor.snippet: only square matrices can be symmetric

annotations.label: Entries symmetric about the main diagonal

anchor.snippet: The entries of a symmetric matrix are symmetric with respect to the main diagonal
the following 3×3 matrix is symmetric
Every square diagonal matrix is symmetric, since all off-diagonal entries are zero

annotations.label: Real symmetric matrix as self-adjoint operator / Hermitian matrix

anchor.snippet: a real symmetric matrix represents a self-adjoint operator over a real inner product space
The symmetric group S n (on a finite set of n symbols) is the group whose elements are all the permutations of the n symbols
it follows that the order (i.e., the number of elements) of the symmetric group S n is n !
A symmetric polynomial is a polynomial P ( X 1 , X 2 , ..., X n ) in n variables
A theorem states that any symmetric polynomial can be expressed in terms of elementary symmetric polynomials
In two variables X 1 and X 2 , one has symmetric polynomials such as
and in three variables X 1 , X 2 and X 3 , one has as a symmetric polynomial
a symmetric tensor is tensor that is invariant under a permutation of its vector arguments
The space of symmetric tensors of rank r on a finite-dimensional vector space is naturally isomorphic to the dual
The central idea of Galois theory is to consider those permutations (or rearrangements) of the roots
an automorphism is an isomorphism from a mathematical object to itself
The set of all automorphisms of an object forms a group , called the automorphism group

annotations.label: Set automorphisms form the symmetric group

anchor.snippet: In set theory , an arbitrary permutation of the elements of a set X is an automorphism
the set of integers , Z , considered as a group under addition, has a unique nontrivial automorphism: negation
A group automorphism is a group isomorphism from a group to itself
an endomorphism of a vector space V is a linear operator V → V
A field automorphism is a bijective ring homomorphism from a field to itself

anchor.section: Symmetry in quantum mechanics: bosons and fermions
anchor.snippet: bosons have representatives that are symmetric under permutation operators, and fermions have antisymmetric representatives

annotations.label: Pauli exclusion equivalent to antisymmetric wavefunction

anchor.section: Symmetry in quantum mechanics: bosons and fermions
anchor.snippet: the Pauli exclusion principle with a single-valued many-particle wavefunction is equivalent to requiring the wavefunction to be antisymmetric

anchor.section: Symmetry in quantum mechanics: bosons and fermions
anchor.snippet: Conversely, if the diagonal quantities A ( x , x ) are zero in every basis
We call a relation symmetric if every time the relation stands from A to B, it stands too from B to A
An isometry is a distance -preserving map between metric spaces
two geometric figures are congruent if they are related by an isometry
A symmetry of a differential equation is a transformation that leaves the differential equation invariant

annotations.label: Line (Lie) symmetry of a system of differential equations

anchor.snippet: A Line symmetry of a system of differential equations is a continuous symmetry of the system of differential equations
knowledge of an appropriate set of Lie symmetries allows one to explicitly calculate a set of first integrals

annotations.label: Permutation symmetry gives discrete uniform distribution

anchor.snippet: symmetry with respect to permutations (relabelings) implies a discrete uniform distribution

annotations.label: Interval symmetry gives continuous uniform distribution

anchor.snippet: symmetry with respect to interchanging sub-intervals of equal length corresponds to a continuous uniform distribution

annotations.label: Reflection in a point preserving a distribution

anchor.snippet: There is one type of isometry in one dimension that may leave the probability distribution unchanged, that is reflection in a point
the outcome and its reciprocal have the same distribution
